title: VAP Scope Amendment
description: Sent when an agent legitimately needs to widen scope or budget mid-session (normal re-planning). Re-baselines the active Scope Commitment. The server MUST semantically re-verify an amendment (a deliberate, audited re-invocation of the costly check) and MAY accept, downgrade, deny, or require human approval. This is the ONLY legitimate path to widen scope; silent escape of the committed envelope is treated as drift. Like the commitment, an amendment is tool-agnostic: it widens the public tool namespace (add_scope) and/or raises universal budget meters (increase_budget) -- it never encodes a tool's argument semantics.

prev_commitment_digest.description: Digest of the commitment (or prior amendment) this one supersedes. Forms a tamper-evident chain. Format 'sha256:<hex>'.

add_scope.description: Public tool-namespace scope to ADD. Same structure as Scope Commitment 'scope' -- tools_allow / tools_deny patterns only (no argument-level semantics). Amendments may only widen via add_scope and increase via increase_budget; narrowing uses replace semantics in a new commitment.

increase_budget.description: Additional budget requested on top of the current commitment. Generic and unit-agnostic, mirroring the commitment budget: 'add_calls' raises max_calls, 'extend_deadline' moves the deadline, and 'add_limits' is a map of named meters to the amount to add to each corresponding 'limits' ceiling.

add_limits.description: Map of named meters to the amount to ADD to each meter's ceiling in budget.limits (e.g. {"usd_opcost": 5, "tokens": 10000}). Mirrors scope_commitment.budget.limits.

reason.description: Why the widening is needed (e.g. 'Dispute volume requires a higher cumulative disbursement ceiling; policy R7 requires escalation'). The semantic judge evaluates this against the session goal.

new_plan_digest.description: Digest of the agent's updated plan after re-planning. Format 'sha256:<hex>'.

signature.description: JWS over the canonicalized amendment. RECOMMENDED for all amendments (they change the trust envelope); REQUIRED when require_signed=true.
